Notes: Kitchen takes celiac seriously. Staff trained. Ask for GF menu at seating. Confirmed with chef in 2024.
*Regular rotation. Nearly everything GF by default. Owner has celiac family member. Ask to confirm sauces.
GF pasta available but kitchen is not dedicated. Go at off-peak for lower cross-contact risk. Not for bad-flare periods.
*GF bun available, dedicated fryer for GF fries. Staff reliable. Good for Nate outings.
Rice-based dishes are GF. Dal, tikka, biryani. Confirm no wheat in marinade each visit. Good standby for Sunday takeout.
Some GF pasta. Kitchen is mixed. Not worth the risk unless eating simple protein and vegetables. Weekend crowds make staff communication harder.
Corn tortillas, GF-flagged menu. Cross-contact possible at chips station. Ask staff to change gloves. We eat here semi-regularly -- has been fine.
All wheat, all sourdough. Obviously not for eating. Sometimes stop to talk shop with the bakers.
Full GF menu available. Staff well-trained. Good for business dinners.
Upscale, attentive. Best for anniversary or special occasion. Called ahead for reservations -- chef confirmed all protocols.
Ceviche, rice dishes, most proteins naturally GF. Avoid fried items -- shared oil. Good Sunday option.
Soy sauce in most dishes. Cross-contact risk high. Added here as a reminder to avoid. February 2025 flare traced here (likely).
